channel.title: c++ on Dorin Fodor
channel.description: Recent content in c++ on Dorin Fodor

item.title: C++: Building a DNS query tool - What I learned
item.description: Preface So I made this CLI tool which lets you query for a domain name and it returns one or more IPs. It’s pretty much just an interface for your DNS server (8.8.8.8, 1.1.1.1, etc.). Check it out here: https://github.com/dorin131/dns-query It only has your standard libraries which are probably included with your OS (Linux), so compiling it should not be a challenge. You would tell it: ./dns fodor.org And you should get back something like:

item.title: C++: Using 3rd party libraries in QT Creator
item.description: My first web request While in the very early stages of building Dorin Browser, I got to the point when I had to implement a web request functionality that would let it fetch resources from the internet. Being a total newbie when it comes to C++, I had no idea how to do this. I was still half way through my “The C++ Programming Language” book by Bjarne Stroustrup, and there was no mention yet on how to do this.
